threat.title: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in Flask Jinja2 Templates
threat.description: When user input is directly embedded into Jinja2 templates without proper escaping, attackers can inject template directives to execute arbitrary Python code on the server, leading to remote code execution.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies user input field that renders in template
2. Injects Jinja2 template payload: {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('whoami').read()}}
3. Flask renders template with malicious code
4. Python code executes on server
5. Attacker gains remote code execution, can read files, execute commands
prerequisites: User input is rendered in Jinja2 template without escaping
Application uses render_template_string() with user input
Flask server has permissions to execute system commands

real_world_breaches.root_cause: Template injection in Flask admin panel

bug_bounty_reports.summary: SSTI leading to remote code execution in Flask application

security_research.title: Server-Side Template Injection: RCE for the Modern Web App
security_research.key_finding: SSTI affects majority of template engines including Jinja2, leading to RCE

mitigations.title: Never Use render_template_string() with User Input
mitigations.description: Avoid rendering templates from strings containing user input. Use predefined template files instead.
validation_steps: Replace with render_template() and template files
Test with SSTI payloads (should not execute)

mitigations.title: Enable Jinja2 Autoescape for All Templates
mitigations.description: Configure Jinja2 to automatically escape all variables by default.

code_example.code:
from flask import Flask
from jinja2 import Environment, select_autoescape

app = Flask(__name__)

# Enable autoescape for all templates
app.jinja_env.autoescape = select_autoescape(
    enabled_extensions=('html', 'xml', 'jinja2'),
    default_for_string=True,
    default=True
)

# Alternatively, set in Flask config
app.config['TEMPLATES_AUTO_RELOAD'] = True
app.jinja_env.auto_reload = True

# Now all variables are auto-escaped
@app.route('/safe')
def safe_route():
    user_input = request.args.get('input', '')
    # user_input is automatically escaped in template
    return render_template('page.html', data=user_input)

validation_steps: Test with HTML/JavaScript injection (should be escaped)
Verify template output in browser DevTools
Use |safe filter ONLY when absolutely necessary

mitigations.title: Implement Sandboxed Template Environment
mitigations.description: Use Jinja2's SandboxedEnvironment to restrict available functions and attributes.
validation_steps: Test SSTI payloads (should fail with SecurityError)
Whitelist only necessary filters and functions

indicators: HTTP 500 errors with Jinja2 syntax errors
Requests with template syntax in parameters ({{, {%, etc.)

log_queries.query: event.category: "web" AND http.request.body.content: ("{{" OR "{%" OR "__class__" OR "__init__" OR "popen")
